---
title: "Intro to R for Decision Modeling"
---

## Learning objectives

By the end of these modules, participants should be able to:

- Create vectors and data frames.
- Write simple functions.
- Use probability distributions for model inputs.
- Read model inputs from CSV files.
- Run simple simulations.
- Summarize model outputs.

## Module 1: R basics

Topics:

- Objects
- Vectors
- Data frames
- Indexing
- Functions

## Module 2: Health economic model inputs

Topics:

- Costs
- Utilities
- Probabilities
- Rates
- Discounting

## Module 3: Simulation basics

Topics:

- Random sampling
- Setting seeds
- Repeated simulations
- Storing results

## Module 4: Plotting and summarizing outputs

Topics:

- Summarizing costs and QALYs
- Plotting model outputs
- Interpreting uncertainty
